mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy. This leads to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive equity returns as investors anticipate higher future profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery has been characterized by steady job growth.
failure_condition: A model based on 3-month PAYEMS momentum shows a rolling 3-year rank information coefficient (IC) consistently below 0.02 against 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a strong leading indicator of a recession. When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises significantly, it triggers fears of an economic contraction, causing a broad market sell-off.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts a positive return over the subsequent 60 trading days, indicating the market is ignoring the labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating but the central bank (FEDFUNDS) fails to tighten policy, real interest rates fall. This combination of rising inflation and accommodative monetary policy boosts asset prices by lowering the real discount rate and signaling tolerance for an overheating economy.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS < 0.1
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has been slow to raise rates post-2008.
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition is followed by negative 60-day returns more than 60% of the time, suggesting the market fears the Fed is 'behind the curve' on inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a slow-growth economic regime, positive surprises in real GDP growth (GDPC1) have an outsized impact on investor sentiment. Higher-than-expected GDP growth leads to significant upward revisions of earnings expectations, driving equity prices higher over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery has featured below-trend GDP growth.
failure_condition: The correlation between the quarterly change in GDPC1 and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) during periods of low GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy. This is a leading indicator of lower corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative forward returns for the broad market.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has shown weakness in late 2015/early 2016.
failure_condition: A signal based on the 6-month momentum of INDPRO fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha (Sharpe ratio < -0.3) in a long-short portfolio sort.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown, as housing has a large multiplier effect. When starts fall below a critical threshold, it signals recession risk, prompting a risk-off move in equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1,000 thousands of units
evidence_refs: fact:The 2008 financial crisis was preceded by a collapse in housing starts.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1,000, the S&P 500 has a positive median 60-day forward return, indicating the market is not pricing in recession risk from the housing sector.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) provides a powerful, joint signal of consumer distress. This dual weakening of consumption and employment leads to sharp downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 6-month % change of RSAFS < 1% AND 3-month change of UNRATE > 0.2
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the primary driver of the US economy.
failure_condition: The performance of a strategy based on the interaction signal is not statistically superior to strategies based on standalone RSAFS or UNRATE signals (t-test on returns p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected monthly increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) during a tightening cycle causes an immediate repricing of risk assets. The higher discount rate leads to lower equity valuations, resulting in negative returns over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed initiated rate hikes in December 2015.
failure_condition: In over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day forward returns, suggesting rate hikes are viewed as a confirmation of economic strength.
